Brand: Universal House Rules & Shared Info
Document 1 Content (Rules / Brand Identi:
Brand Master Prompts - One Definitive Prompt Per Brand
Purpose: One copy-paste-ready master prompt per brand that, with a few bracketed swaps, will generate any creative (Meta/IG/LinkedIn ad, story, flyer, business card, blog header, conference banner, merch mockup) on-brand for that brand.
This file consolidates the two source docs into a single canonical reference:
AD_CREATIVE_MASTER_PROMPTS.md ad ideology pulled from LS DESIGN PRODUCTIONS/
brand-kits/BRAND KIT MASTER PROMPTS.md-brand-identity ideology pulled from Brand Kits/
Each section gives identity, palette, type, photo direction, layout rules, headline & CTA patterns, and one comprehensive master prompt.

Universal house rules (applied beneath every brand below)
• Aspect ratios shipped per asset: Feed 1:1, Instagram 4:5, Story 9:16, YouTube/Google 16:9, display 300x250 / 370x300, flyer US Letter 8.5x11, business card 3.5x2 in.
• Layout grammar: Logo top-left or top-center -> bold left-stacked headline -> 1-2 line sub-headline -> coloured CTA pill-> photographic subject occupying the right 40-50% (or bottom-right third).
• Typography: Bold geometric sans for headlines (Causten on Legal Soft brands, Clash Display on Practice 360, brand-specific sans elsewhere). Two-tone headline trick: brand-accent colour on the most important 2-3 words, white or near-black on the rest.
• Photography rule: Al-generated or stock photos of professional Filipino / Latina / Caucasian / multi-ethnic remote workers wearing business attire and slim black headsets, lit warm but cool-toned, smiling gently, with direct eye contact, cleanly cut out or composited over the brand-colour gradient.
• Persona-led headlines: Most ads open with a vocative - Lawyers: Attorneys:, Law Firm Owners: followed by a pain question or value statement.
• Offer/price highlight: Dollar amounts ($10/hr, Save up to 80%, $500 Off) are always the largest in-image text and sit inside a coloured pill.
• Sub-brand lockup rule: Every Legal Soft sub-brand carries a small powered by LEGAL SOFT micro-line beneath its own wordmark.
• Export filename pattern: <seq>_Image_<bgColor>_<textColor>_<intentTag>_<brand>_<MMDDYY>.png where intentTag {ValueProp, Offer, EventAttendees, Promo, Discount}.
Document 2 Content (Sales / Visual / Cop:
LS Design Productions - Ad Creative Master Prompts
Reverse-engineered visual and copy ideology of every brand under LS Design Productions, written as ready-to-use master prompts for an image generator (Midjourney / DALL-E / Sora / Stable Diffusion) so you can replicate the exact ad style for each brand.
Each section gives:
1. Brand identity - what the brand is and who it sells to
2. Visual ideology - what every ad does the same way
3. Copy ideology - voice, structure, and call-to-action style
4. Master Prompt - copy-paste prompt template with bracketed slots
5. Reference assets observed in this library

Universal house style (applies to every Legal/VA brand below)
These rules are visible across every Legal Soft sub-brand. Start here, then layer the brand-specific overrides below.
• Aspect ratios shipped per ad: Feed 1:1, Instagram 4:5, Story 9:16, YouTube/Google 16:9, plus 300x250/370x300 display banners.
• Layout grammar: Logo top-center or top-left -> punchy headline left-aligned -> 1-2 line sub-headline in lighter weight -> pill-shaped CTA button -> photographic subject occupying the right or bottom-right third.
• Typography: Bold sans-serif for headlines (Causten / similar geometric sans). The most important 2-3 words are colored in the brand accent; the rest is white or near-black depending on background brightness.
• Photography rule: Al-generated or stock photos of professional Filipino/Latina/Caucasian remote workers wearing business attire and headsets, lit warm but cool-toned, in modern offices or against soft brand-color gradients. Subjects make direct eye contact and smile gently.
• Persona-led headlines: Most ads open with a vocative - "Lawyers:", "Attorneys:", "Law Firm Owners:" - followed by a pain question or a value proposition.
• Offer/price highlight: Dollar amounts (e.g. $12/hr, Save up to 80%, $1,000 OFF) are always the largest in-image text apart from the headline and sit inside a colored pill.
• CTA labels: Book a Demo, Get Started, See Candidates, Explore Our Talent, Save Your Spot. Always inside a high-contrast pill (orange/ dark navy/black).
• Brand footprint: Logo always at top or bottom; never both. White when over a dark/colored background, full color when over a light background.
• Naming convention encoded in filenames: <n>_Image_<bgColor>_<textColor>_<intentTag>_<brand>_<MMDDYY>.png where intentTag E ValueProp | Offer | EventAttendees | ScaleBookkeeping50PercentOff. Use the same scheme when exporting.

Brand: 1. Legal Soft - Flagship virtual legal staffing
Document 1 Content (Rules / Brand Identi:
Identity: End-to-end virtual legal staff (paralegals, intake, case managers, attorneys, lien negotiators) for U.S. law firms.
Palette: Deep navy #0A2B6B #1E55D6 #2A6DF4 on white; orange #FF7A1A reserved for CTA pills; optional warm orange light-leak top-left on event creatives.
Type: Causten Font Family (LS new Brand Kit/Causten Font Family/).
Logo: LEGAL SOFT wordmark with an upward bar-chart icon between the two words. White on dark, navy on light.
Photography: Smiling professional (woman in royal-blue blazer + headset OR business-casual professional at a laptop) cut out on the right; or 4-up grid of diverse remote-staff headshots floating over a blue gradient panel.
Headline patterns: Lawyers: ..., Attorneys: Hire Virtual Legal Staff For Your Firm!, Have an influx of cases?, Already have another virtual staff provider? We'll match or beat their price., conference variant Legal Soft goes to [EVENT NAME].
CTA labels: Book a Demo, Get a Quote, Month-to-month, cancel any time, Visit Us at Booth [N].
Decorative motifs: Dotted halftone arcs in the lower-right corner; faint X / arrow pattern in event backgrounds.
Master prompt
A [ASPECT 1:1 | 4:5 | 9:16 | 16:9] Legal Soft creative for [USE CASE Meta feed ad | IG story | conference IG square | save-the-date post | display banner | business card].
Background: deep navy gradient (#0A2B6B center #1E55D6 edges) on white panels, with a subtle blue mesh/halftone texture and a small cluster of dotted halftone arcs in the bottom-right corner. For event/conference creatives, replace the gradient with a duotone architectural photograph of the event city ([NYC skyscrapers shot looking up / San Diego marina with the curved Marriott towers / Denver mountains]) in deep navy with a soft warm orange light leak top-left.
Top-left logo: small white-or-navy "LEGAL SOFT" wordmark with the rising bar-chart icon between the two words; on conference creatives, add the partner event lockup top-right (e.g. "TBI MED LEGAL CONFERENCE", "PILMMA SUPER SUMMIT", "ALM | LAW.COM LEGALWEEK 2025" in white outline) and add the white connector word "goes to" centred between them.
Headline (left-stacked, bold Causten / geometric sans, deep navy on white or white on navy, -64pt, 3-4 short lines): "[QUESTION OR HOOK HEADLINE -e.g 'Have an influx of cases?' or 'Hire Virtual Legal Staff For Your Firm!']".
Sub-headline (lighter weight, smaller, dark gray on white or light-blue on navy): "[ONE-LINE SOLUTION e.g. 'Instantly hire more legal staff trained in your practice area and tech stack.']" (for event creatives, replace with "Visit Us at Booth [NUMBER]").
Offer pill (vibrant orange #FF7A1A, rounded, white sans text, bottom-left or bottom-right): "[$ AMOUNT Off ROLE NAME]" e.g. "$500 Off PI Trained Case Manager", or "Month-to-month, cancel any time".
Hero visual (right 40-50%):
Option A single photoreal smiling professional [woman in royal-blue blazer with white shirt and a black headset / Latina in tan blouse working on a silver MacBook], cut out, soft cinematic rim light.
Option B 4-up cluster of floating rounded-square cards, each containing a photoreal portrait of a diverse remote legal staff worker (Asian woman, Latino man, Caucasian woman, Filipino man all in dark blazers + slim black headsets, gently smiling), arranged over a solid bright blue (#2A6DF4) rounded panel.
For business cards (3.5x2 in, 600 dpi, front+back artboards): solid black ground; front gold (#C9A861) Causten name + light-gray italic title + thin white sans contact stack; back: centred white Legal Soft arrow logomark with "LEGALSOFT.COM" beneath. Bleed marks included.
For the bottom: thin white divider + small caption "[DATE RANGE] | [VENUE]" on event creatives; "LEGALSOFT.COM" on transactional. Style: clean modern B2B SaaS / legal marketing, premium, slightly conversion-focused.

Identity: Hamid Kohan, founder/CEO of Legal Soft. Editorial press features, TechTimes-style layouts, podcast covers, conference keynote graphics.
Palette: Editorial neutrals (warm grays, ivory, charcoal).
Photography: Professional studio headshot in a dark suit; or on-stage keynote shots.
Master prompt
A [ASPECT -1:1 | 16:9 | 4:5] Hamid Kohan personal-brand creative for [USE CASE press feature graphic | podcast cover | keynote announcement | LinkedIn post].
Background: editorial cream (#F6F2EA) or charcoal (#1F1F1F) with a faint horizontal ruled-line motif (like a magazine pull-quote spread).
Layout: split left two-thirds is type, right one-third is a cut-out studio headshot of Hamid Kohan in a dark suit, slight smile, looking forward, soft natural-window light.
Pull-quote (left, charcoal or cream bold serif, 60pt, 3 lines with hanging quotation marks): "[QUOTE -e.g '"The future of legal practice is virtual-first."']".
Byline (smaller sans, under quote): "Hamid Kohan, Founder & CEO, Legal Soft".
Optional publication lockup top-left: "[Featured in TECHTIMES / FORBES / LAW360]" in a thin rule.
CTA pill (optional, if for an event): coral or orange rounded pill, white text e.g. 'Hear Hamid speak at [EVENT]')" bottom-right.
Aspect ratio [1:1 | 16:9 | 4:5]. Style: premium editorial / press-feature feel.

Brand: Misc / Master Prompts and Export Guidelines
Document 1 Content (Rules / Brand Identi:
How to use these prompts
1. Pick the brand section that matches your asset.
2. Copy the master prompt block.
3. Fill every [BRACKETED] slot with the campaign / asset specifics.
4. Pick the aspect ratio from the [ASPECT ...] choices.
5. (Optional) Adda --style rawv 6-ar [ratio] suffix for Midjourney, or paste straight into DALL E/Imagen / Nano-Banana / Reve / Sora image mode.
Each prompt is self-contained - colour codes, type, photo direction, layout, headline pattern, CTA copy and decorative motifs are all baked in, so a fresh design pass should land on-brand even without the human designer in the loop.

Sources
All identity, palette, type, photography, and layout calls in this document are reverse-engineered from the actual asset files in:
LS DESIGN PRODUCTIONS/ production ad creatives (304 files) Brand Kits/ master brand identity assets (153 files)
See:
AD_CREATIVE_MASTER_PROMPTS.md for the per-creative reference asset paths.
brand-kits/BRAND KIT MASTER PROMPTS.md for the per-kit reference asset paths.
folder_summary.csv for the full 283-row folder-by-folder inventory.
Document 2 Content (Sales / Visual / Cop:
Quick "fill-in-the-blanks" master meta-prompt
Use this if you want one prompt that adapts to any brand in this library:
A [ASPECT] Meta/LinkedIn ad for "[BRAND NAME]" [ONE-LINE BRAND DESCRIPTION].
Background: [BRAND ACCENT GRADIENT or SOLID].
Top-[LEFT|CENTER]: [BRAND LOGO LOCKUP] in [white brand color], ~24-30pt.
Headline (left-aligned, bold geometric sans, 60pt, 2-3 stacked lines): "[PERSONA HOOK e.g. 'Lawyers: '] / [PAIN-OR-VALUE STATEMENT]" split the most important 2-3 words in the brand accent color, the rest in white or dark navy depending on background brightness.
Sub-headline (regular sans, smaller, lighter shade): "[ONE TACTICAL SENTENCE delivering the solution or proof point]"
Offer pill (large, rounded, brand accent color): "[PRICE OR OFFER e.g. 'Starting at $12/hr', 'Save Up to 80%', '$1,000 OFF Your First Month']"
Hero photo (right/bottom-right ~40% of frame, rounded corners or floated cards): a single photoreal portrait or 3-4 cluster of diverse remote staff workers wearing professional attire and slim black headsets, gently smiling at camera, lit warm against [BRAND COLOR GRADIENT]. AI-generated, hyperreal, slight cinematic rim light.
CTA pill (high-contrast vs background, rounded, bottom-right): "[CTA e.g. 'Book a Demo', 'Get Started', 'Explore Our Talent', 'See Candidates']" orange/black/gold depending on brand.
Decorative motifs (subtle, per brand): [dotted halftone arcs | scattered color squares | honeycomb hex mesh | floating glassmorphic UI cards | bar chart panels].
Output as [1:1 | 4:5 | 9:16 | 16:9 | 300x250 banner]. Style: clean modern B2B SaaS / legal marketing, premium, slightly conversion-focused.

File-naming convention to keep when exporting
The LS Design team encodes every ad's intent right in the filename. Mirror this:
<sequenceNumber>_Image_<BackgroundColor>_<TextColor>_<IntentTag>_<BrandName>_<MMDDYY>.png
Examples observed:
1_Image_White_Text_ValueProp_Legal Soft_063025.png
3a_Image_Gradient_Text_Offer_Bookkeeper_100825.png
5_Image_Blue_Text_ValueProp_Legal Soft Bookkeeping.law_070125.png
2A_LegalAssistant_Image_White_Text_EventAttendees + Offer_TVLA_091225.png
Where:
• sequenceNumber = batch order (1, 2, 3a, 3b, 5b...)
• BackgroundColor = White | Blue | Gradient | Image
• TextColor = Text (default) or White Text
• IntentTag = ValueProp | Offer | EventAttendees | Promo | Discount
• BrandName = exact LS brand name
• MMDDYY = creation/export date
